title: DOB Construction Code + amendment-overlay corpus (official PDFs, scope-bounded; Local Law / DOB Rule / Buildings Bulletin / Code Note effective-date overlay)
objective: PROPOSED — NOT CONTRACTED, and BLOCKED until the owner-approved construction-code release-scope artifact exists (B-011). Ingest official DOB chapter PDFs, ONLY within the owner-approved release scope (docs/CONSTRUCTION_CODE_RELEASE_SCOPE.md); build an effective-date overlay for subsequent Local Laws, DOB Rules, Buildings Bulletins, relevant Code Notes/directives, and rescissions/supersessions (record issuing body, number, date, cited provisions, status, rescission/supersession for each). UpCodes is NEVER the source corpus — manual discrepancy-spotting only, resolved against official sources; never controlling provenance; no runtime dependency. The official DOB page warns that not all applicable laws are on its website: this corpus NEVER claims universal legal completeness; it reports its declared scope and routes unresolved/out-of-scope applicability to a licensed professional. PDF extraction is a SEPARATE bounded /dependency-security decision (prefer text-bearing official PDFs; OCR draft-only with page/image provenance). REUSE (import read-only) M3-T002's ingest/parser/fidelity core; own only construction_code/** + overlay/**. FIXTURE-vs-PRODUCTION invariant (item 3): fixture/parser/harness work may be developed and reviewed while B-001 blocks durable storage, but this task does NOT reach 'accepted' for any construction-code scope, does NOT mark the construction-code domain 'implemented', and does NOT unblock any feasibility claim until durable content-addressed official captures + overlay evidence exist (enforced by B-001 in blockers[]). Full spec: project-control/reports/M3-CORPUS-REPLAN-PROPOSAL.md.
business_reason: Any construction-feasibility claim (egress, occupancy, structural, accessibility, fire/life-safety) depends on an official construction-code corpus with amendment overlays; it is currently entirely missing. Until the relevant approved scope is accepted with durable captures, such claims must be gated professional_review_required, and no envelope output may be relabeled feasible/buildable on construction-code grounds.
inputs: docs/CONSTRUCTION_CODE_RELEASE_SCOPE.md APPROVED by owner (B-011 cleared) — code edition + as-of date; product claims supported; exact in-scope titles/chapters/domains; required LL/DOB-Rule/Bulletin/Code-Note/rescission/supersession channels; excluded domains; consumer claim each exclusion blocks

M3-T001 accepted: SOURCE_AUTHORITY_POLICY.md (tier 1 Construction Code, tier 3 DOB Rules/Bulletins/Code Notes/formal interpretations), LEGAL_CORPUS_COVERAGE_MATRIX.md (construction-code + accessibility/fire-egress/structural domains + actionable columns), the new SOURCE_ACCESS_REGISTRY channels, legal_source_manifest schema

M3-T002 accepted AS PRODUCTION CORPUS + its exported ingest/parser/fidelity core (services/api/app/corpus/ingest|parsers|fidelity) — IMPORT read-only; REUSE the fetch/normalize/hash/manifest machinery + app.resilience.transport; do not fork or mutate

Official DOB Construction Code chapter PDFs + LL/DOB-Rule/Buildings-Bulletin/Code-Note channels (identities recorded in the M3-T001 registry additions; exact download URLs 'to verify at G1' — nyc.gov bot-wall 403 to non-browser clients, human browser session may be required; an unresolved identity is BLOCKED at G1, never accepted as verified)
B-001 (Supabase management token) + approved storage/ingestion infrastructure — gates durable storage AND this task's acceptance; fixtures-only engineering path if unavailable
B-011 (owner-approved construction-code release scope) — gates readiness
outputs: services/api/app/corpus/construction_code/** — scope-bounded chapter ingestion (reusing M3-T002 core) + scope-boundary guard that routes out-of-scope domains to professional_review_required (OWNED by M3-T004)
services/api/app/corpus/overlay/** — Local Law / DOB Rule / Buildings Bulletin / Code Note effective-date + rescission/supersession overlay resolver (OWNED by M3-T004)
services/api/tests/corpus/construction_code/** — deterministic scope-boundary + overlay + PDF-replay tests on frozen fixtures
services/api/tests/fixtures/corpus/construction_code/** — bounded frozen official PDF fixtures (NOT the full code)
forbidden_paths: services/api/app/corpus/ingest/**, parsers/**, fidelity/**, versioning/** (owned by M3-T002 — import read-only, never mutate), services/api/app/corpus/closure/** (owned by M3-T003)
Using UpCodes (or any third-party service) as the source corpus or controlling provenance, or adding a runtime dependency on it

Ingesting beyond the owner-approved release scope; claiming universal legal completeness; emitting any construction-feasibility conclusion without routing unresolved/out-of-scope applicability to professional review
Silently adding a PDF/OCR package — PDF/OCR is a separate /dependency-security decision; OCR output never treated as verified; marking the construction-code domain 'implemented' on fixtures alone
Committing bulk/citywide code corpus into git or leaving bulk temp data on the owner PC; fetching live inside a per-property analysis
Rule publication/Verified labeling; editing canonical contracts; apps/web/**; project-control/** except own reports; .claude/** except own agent-memory
acceptance_preconditions: READINESS (backlog -> ready) requires B-011 cleared (owner-approved docs/CONSTRUCTION_CODE_RELEASE_SCOPE.md) and M3-T001 + M3-T002 accepted.

ACCEPTANCE (not gate-PASS) additionally requires: B-001 resolved; durable content-addressed official Construction-Code + amendment-overlay captures exist; overlay evidence (effective-date + rescission/supersession) recorded against those durable captures.

Until then, fixture/parser/harness increments may PASS G0-G5 as an engineering increment but the task remains awaiting_gate/blocked (B-001), is NOT accepted for any construction-code scope, does NOT mark the construction-code domain 'implemented', and does NOT unblock any feasibility claim.
acceptance_scenarios: AS-1 scope-bounded ingest: an in-scope Construction Code chapter PDF ingests with full provenance (publisher/tier, URL, timestamp, raw+normalized SHA-256, chapter/section hierarchy, effective date, parser version, extraction status) via the M3-T002 machinery.

AS-2 amendment overlay: a subsequent Local Law / DOB Rule / Buildings Bulletin / Code Note is recorded with issuing body, number, date, cited provisions, status, and rescission/supersession, and correctly overlays the base chapter's effective text as-of a date.

AS-3 declared-scope honesty: the corpus advertises its owner-approved scope; an out-of-scope domain query returns not_evaluated/professional_review_required (routing the exact consumer claim the scope artifact says that exclusion blocks), never a completeness claim.
AS-4 no universal-completeness claim: a machine check asserts no output labels the corpus universally complete; the DOB 'not all applicable laws are on this website' caveat is recorded verbatim.
AS-5 UpCodes discipline: a discrepancy spotted via UpCodes is recorded as a lead resolved against an official source; UpCodes never appears as provenance and adds no runtime dependency.
AS-6 PDF extraction provenance: text-bearing PDF extraction replays to the source page; if OCR is used it is flagged draft with page/image provenance and cannot feed a Verified result.

AS-7 fixture-not-production (item 3): with B-001 open, a machine/report check confirms the construction-code domain is NOT marked 'implemented', the task is NOT accepted for any scope, and no feasibility claim is unblocked — only the engineering increment is reviewed.

AS-8 scope artifact gates readiness (item 6): a G0 check confirms docs/CONSTRUCTION_CODE_RELEASE_SCOPE.md is owner-approved (B-011 cleared) before the task can move to ready; without it the task stays backlog/blocked.
NC-1 rescission respected: a rescinded/superseded provision is not served as current; the overlay marks it and points to the superseding instrument.
NC-2 feasibility gate: no envelope/scenario output is relabeled feasible/buildable/compliant on construction-code grounds while the relevant M3-T004 scope is unaccepted.
NC-3 bot-wall honesty: an unverifiable download URL behind the nyc.gov 403 wall is marked 'to verify at G1 via human browser session' and BLOCKED, never guessed or scraped around.
risks: PROPOSAL ONLY + BLOCKED: no dispatch until owner approves the packet + dependency order; readiness additionally requires B-011 (owner-approved release scope).

FIXTURE != PRODUCTION: gate PASS on fixtures is an engineering increment; acceptance for any construction-code scope (and 'implemented' status, and unblocking feasibility) requires durable captures + overlay evidence — enforced by B-001 in blockers[].
DEPENDS ON PRODUCTION M3-T002: requires M3-T002 accepted as durable production corpus; a fixture-only M3-T002 does not satisfy it.
SCOPE CREEP: ingest ONLY the owner-approved release-scope domains; the full construction code is out of scope and must not be attempted or implied complete.
PDF/OCR DEPENDENCY: separate bounded /dependency-security decision; prefer text-bearing PDFs; OCR draft-only with provenance.

BOT WALL: www.nyc.gov / www1.nyc.gov return 403 to non-browser clients; download-URL enumeration may need a human browser session — never scrape around restrictions; unresolved identity BLOCKED at G1.
requirement_refs: docs/CONSTRUCTION_CODE_RELEASE_SCOPE.md (owner-approved scope; drafted by M3-T001; gated by B-011)
docs/SOURCE_ACCESS_REGISTRY.md (new legal channels added in M3-T001; cross-source bot-wall note)
harness_assignments: SOURCE-FIDELITY HARNESS (reused from M3-T002, extended to PDF page replay + amendment-overlay as-of resolution + rescission/supersession).

CONSUMER-BOUNDARY HARNESS (owner directive section 6, construction-code slice): M5/report consumers must not relabel any output feasible/buildable/compliant on construction-code grounds while the relevant scope is unaccepted; out-of-scope domains return professional_review_required routing the exact consumer claim named in the scope artifact.
